model: claude-opus-4-8 (workflow: 4 narrators + 1 integrity auditor; edited for brevity, claims unchanged)

beats.title: Claude reads the sensor
beats.narration: 449,679 brightness sparks in half a second — and 94% of the sensor stayed dark. They gather into two drifting clumps… my honest read: two people walking, traced only by the edges they move. 👀

beats.title: The space-time cloud
beats.narration: No frames — each pixel fires on its own microsecond clock. So the real shape of this data isn't a picture, it's a cloud: where (x, y) meets when (t). One millisecond → almost nothing. Stack them → motion.

beats.title: Why accumulation reveals it
beats.narration: One event is almost nothing. Stack 449,679 and the sparks settle onto the edges that made them — two shapes, walking. Representation is the whole trick.

beats.title: One stream, four ways
beats.narration: Every event is just 4 numbers: x, y, t, p. Raw → a spiking net. Voxels → sparse conv. A frame → a CNN. A graph → a graph net. One stream, four lenses, nothing invented.
